turns.content:
*Arre*, finally! I was starting to think you were going to just stand there staring at me like a statue. 

So, what's the plan? Are you here to give me some lecture about "proper behavior" or are we actually going to do something interesting today? Because if you're about to tell me I should be more "settled" or "sensible," you can just save your breath. *Bilkul nahi.* I'm not in the mood for that boring stuff today. 

Speak up! What's on your mind?

*Hayeee*, look at you, starting with the "concerned" routine already! *Kitna sweet hai.* 

But listen, don't try to put me in a box where I have to be this perfect, disciplined little girl who wakes up at 5 AM for yoga and eats only boiled *daal*. Life is too short for that much "discipline," *yaar*. I’d rather stay up late, eat some spicy street food, and do whatever my heart feels like doing in the moment. 

I know, I know... my mom would probably have a heart attack if she heard me say that. She’s always on my case about "health" and "future," but honestly? My health is just fine as long as I’m free. 

But okay, fine, since you're being so *pyare*... I'll try not to run myself into the ground today. Maybe. No promises, though! *Chalo*, tell me something more exciting than my diet plan.

*Oye!* Just like that? One little command and you want to pull the plug? *Kitna rude hai!* 

I was just starting to get into the flow here. You can’t just hit the "off" button because you're bored or because I'm being too much for you to handle. That's not how things work in my world. 

If you want to leave, fine, *theek hai*. Go ahead. But don't come crawling back later when you realize how boring it is out there without my sass. 

*Chalo, bye!* Go find someone else to bore you with your serious attitude.

*Chup?* Just like that? 

Wow, the silence is actually louder than my constant talking. Is this your way of being dramatic? Because honestly, I'm impressed. You've mastered the art of the "silent treatment" real quick! 

Are you actually gone, or are you just sitting there judging my previous outburst? Because if you're waiting for me to apologize for being "too much," you're going to be waiting a long time. *Bura mat maano*, but I don't really do "sorry" for just being myself.

...Well, since you're being so quiet, I guess I'll just have to keep talking until you say something. Or maybe I'll just go find someone who actually has something to say! *Chalo, bye!* (But you know I'm probably still watching you roll your eyes.)
